decision_summary: The runtime-generated diagnosis path now proves that upstream allocation and policy evidence can be converted into a concrete follow-up task.
rationale: The latest runtime proof emits a council review packet with diagnosis category `coverage-gap` and explicit allocation, policy, resource, and team-output evidence refs. That diagnosis recommends expanding the auditable loop shape across additional backend families. The benchmark then uses the existing task-open surface to materialize that recommendation as TASK-034 while preserving orchestrator session and decision provenance. This is sufficient to prove that diagnosis can leave the narrative layer and become an actionable runtime follow-up artifact.
recommendation: Accept this runtime-generated follow-up task trace as DG-004 evidence and next extend the same pattern to additional diagnosis families.
target_audience: A framework operator deciding whether diagnosis surfaces in AOF can produce action instead of only explanation.
expected_user_reaction: The operator should be able to inspect one diagnosis packet and one task artifact and see a direct line from upstream cause to follow-up work.
artifact_change_recommendations: Keep diagnosis evidence refs and follow-up task provenance bound together when benchmarking organizational causes.
Prefer follow-up tasks whose titles clearly reflect the diagnosed upstream gap.
organization_change_recommendations: Promote diagnosis-to-task generation into a first-class runtime pattern rather than an ad hoc operator step.
Reuse the same benchmark shape for role-gap and loop-gap diagnosis families.
